title: First-party Neovim workflow module map
summary: The repository is a personal Neovim system whose custom modules extend beyond the C++ frontend into authoring, diagnostics, navigation, profiling, assembly, project indexing, protection, menus, and Julia presentation.
details: init.lua loads config options, keymaps, autocmds, lazy.nvim plugin specs, then first-party frontend, authoring, assembly, probe, code-generation, diagnostics, profiling, protection, and Julia modules.
cpp_doc_markdown renders hpp triple-slash blocks; dans_macros discovers real project defines; dans_diagmark compresses diagnostics to first-cell and line-number marks.

cpp_authoring owns structured templates and dollar expressions; cpp_tools supplies shared compile/context/job primitives; dans_asm, cpp_runner, and cpp_codegen provide assembly, probes, and parser-derived generation.

cpp_tools/project.lua is the Neovim client for a standalone deterministic C++ physical-project index covering inferred components, direct include provenance, compiler profiles, CMake targets, target consumers, dependencies, and cycles.
dans_perf provides event-loop responsiveness and LuaJIT sampling tools; dans_keylog records a bounded key history; dans_jumptrail formats the statusline jumplist path.

dans_protect marks external C/C++/CUDA files and configured paths read-only; dans_mode owns the global frontend and requested/effective monochrome state while dans_vanilla remains only a compatibility facade.
dans_menu is the canonical owner-facing palette for display and font controls, authoring, assembly, function probes, C++ generation, project inspection, and developer utilities.
Ordinary Markdown uses literal source presentation; VimTeX, texlab, latexmk, and an available SyncTeX-capable viewer provide the LaTeX workflow without syntax conceal or save-time formatting.
julia_scope provides keyword/end and bracket scopes plus ib/ab text objects; julia_progress replaces noisy julials progress cycles with one persistent widget.
lua/custom/plugins contains lazy.nvim plugin specs; config/lazy.lua imports that directory as the single plugin-specification entry point.

provenance.reference: 2026-07-11 initial inventory and 2026-07-12 C++ tooling audit
provenance.note: Responsibilities were derived from current code rather than the archived README.
Current entry point and module trees define the actual layout.

evidence.reference: tests/authoring_spec.lua, cpp_tools_spec.lua, asm_interaction_spec.lua, runner_interaction_spec.lua, codegen_spec.lua, project_index_ui_spec.lua, protect_spec.lua, jumptrail_spec.lua, scope_spec.lua
evidence.claim: Representative authoring, C++ tooling, project inspection, and non-core modules have isolated behavioral coverage.

questions: What does this Neovim configuration contain besides the C++ renderer?
Where are plugin specifications and first-party modules loaded?
Which module owns assembly or file protection?

last_verified.method: Current source tree, setup calls, central palette, display controller, markup policy, language tooling, public APIs, and interaction specs inventoried.
